exercises.title: Benchmark front inserts
exercises.description: Compare repeated front inserts against appends in a dynamic array implementation and note the difference.
exercises.expected_outcome: Front inserts should be much slower than appends because they force shifting.

exercises.title: Trace stack order
exercises.description: Push a small sequence of values onto a stack and pop them to confirm LIFO behavior.
exercises.expected_outcome: The last value pushed should be the first value popped.

exercises.title: Build a circular-buffer queue
exercises.description: Implement enqueue and dequeue with a fixed-capacity circular buffer and handle wraparound correctly.
exercises.expected_outcome: Enqueue and dequeue should both stay O(1) without array shifting.

exercises.title: Reverse a linked list
exercises.description: Write an iterative function that reverses a singly linked list and verify the new head and tail.
exercises.expected_outcome: The list order should be reversed in-place with pointer rewiring only.

exercises.title: Undo/redo with two stacks
exercises.description: Model an editor history using separate undo and redo stacks, then exercise insert, undo, and redo flows.
exercises.expected_outcome: Undo and redo should both work in O(1) per operation.

exercises.title: Bounded work queue benchmark
exercises.description: Build a bounded producer-consumer queue and compare a linked-list implementation with a circular buffer.
exercises.expected_outcome: The circular-buffer version should use less overhead and keep throughput steady.
